title: Neural Network Model Compression via Knowledge Distillation
abstract: A method for compressing neural network models using knowledge distillation techniques. The method involves training a smaller student network to mimic the behavior of a larger teacher network, resulting in reduced model size while maintaining accuracy.

claims.text: A method for compressing a neural network model, comprising: providing a teacher neural network having a first set of parameters; providing a student neural network having a second set of parameters, wherein the second set of parameters is smaller than the first set of parameters; training the student neural network to approximate outputs of the teacher neural network; and deploying the trained student neural network.
The method of claim 1, wherein training the student neural network comprises minimizing a distillation loss between outputs of the teacher network and outputs of the student network.
The method of claim 2, wherein the distillation loss comprises a Kullback-Leibler divergence between softmax outputs of the teacher and student networks.
The method of claim 1, wherein the student neural network has at least 50% fewer parameters than the teacher neural network.

A system for neural network compression, comprising: a processor; and a memory storing instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: receive a teacher neural network model; generate a student neural network model with fewer parameters; train the student model using outputs from the teacher model; and output the trained student model.
The system of claim 5, wherein the instructions further cause the processor to apply temperature scaling to softmax outputs during training.

description:
FIELD OF THE INVENTION

The present invention relates to machine learning, and more particularly to methods and systems for compressing neural network models.

BACKGROUND

Deep neural networks have achieved remarkable success in various domains including computer vision, natural language processing, and speech recognition. However, these models often contain millions or billions of parameters, making them challenging to deploy on resource-constrained devices such as mobile phones and embedded systems.

Knowledge distillation has emerged as a promising technique for model compression. The core idea is to transfer knowledge from a large, complex teacher model to a smaller, more efficient student model.

DETAILED DESCRIPTION

Referring now to the figures, FIG. 1 illustrates an overview of the knowledge distillation process according to an embodiment of the present invention...
